license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.zvwf67e
title: RNA Isolation for Tissue using TRIzol
author: Sze-Xian Lim

steps_raw.instruction: Add 1 mL, TRIzol of TRIzol per 30 mg, tissue of tissue and homogenize using handheld homogenizer.
Incubate at 25 °C for 00:05:00 to allow nucleoprotein complexes to dissociate.
Centrifuge at max speed for 00:15:00 at 4 °C .
Carefully remove the top aqueous phase and transfer to a new Eppendorf tube. The interphase and bottom organic phase can be saved for DNA and protein respectively.
Add 500 µL, 100% isopropanol of 100% isopropanol to the aqueous phase, mix by inversion and incubate at -20 °C for a minimum of 02:00:00 .
Spin down at maximum speed for 30 mins to precipitate RNA.
Remove supernatant, and add 1 mL of 75 % volume, ethanol ethanol to wash pellet.
Add 200 µL, chloroform of chloroform to each ml of TRIzol carefully and vortex to mix well.
Spin down at max speed for 15 minutes and remove supernatant.
Resuspend pellet in appropriate volume of nuclease free water.
